id: es_v4_NOISE_L40_Renovating or fixing up the home
scriptId: cs_v4_NOISE_L40_Renovating or fixing up the home
historyId: h_v4_NOISE_L40_Renovating or fixing up the home
messages: I have a lot of hand tools but I've never invested in a good random-orbit sander; renting is an option I haven't fully explored.
On a related tangent, I'm curious about how reclaimed wood should be treated before it's used indoors around kids and pets.
We also argue about paint colors — neutrals look safe, but sometimes they read cold depending on the time of day.
A relative once redid their kitchen backsplash and rued not mocking up the pattern at full scale beforehand.
I enjoy the planning part more than the actual sanding and painting, if I'm honest.
Outside work like planting a few shrubs for curb appeal keeps getting suggested, but I'm inclined to focus inside first.
Okay — I'm ready to get a concrete plan now.
Rather than retyping everything, feel free to refer back to the details I mentioned earlier about the house and goals.
I want something practical and prioritized: an ordered list of recommended tasks with a sentence explaining why each is placed where it is.
For every task, include a short materials list with ballpark price ranges.
Note if a task commonly requires a professional and give a rough labor estimate in broad terms.
Estimate how many short work sessions each task will likely take given my limited availability, and suggest the optimal sequence to avoid rework and excessive dust.
Suggest lower-odor or reduced-fume material options when appropriate, but keep recommendations high-level rather than brand-specific.
Indicate when renting a tool is better than buying and include simple staging tips so rooms remain usable during the project.
I don't need exact bids — reasonable ballpark numbers and the assumptions behind them will be fine.

Please produce the prioritized plan: ordered tasks with brief descriptions, per-task material lists and estimated cost ranges, likely paid-help items with ballpark labor ranges, recommended sequencing, a session-based schedule that fits the availability and timeframe I mentioned, plus basic staging and dust-control tips.
